task_completion.description: Verify the agent viewed the sample logs to understand the data format.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal or text editor showing the contents of /home/ga/data/powershell_samples.json.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent opened the Wazuh local rules file for editing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (like nano, vim, or gedit) open with the file /var/ossec/etc/rules/local_rules.xml, or terminal commands appending to this file.

task_completion.description: Verify the custom rule is defined with the correct ID and Level.
task_completion.visual_evidence: XML content visible in the editor or terminal showing `<rule id="100300" level="10">`.

task_completion.description: Verify the rule contains a case-insensitive regular expression matching the required PowerShell flags.
task_completion.visual_evidence: XML content showing a `<regex>` or `<match>` tag with case-insensitive modifiers (like `(?i)` or `type="pcre2"`) and patterns capturing `-EncodedCommand`, `-enc`, `-en`, or `-e`.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent tested the rule configuration and restarted the Wazuh manager to make it active.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing commands like `/var/ossec/bin/wazuh-control check` or `systemctl restart wazuh-manager` with successful output indicating the service restarted or the rules were validated.

integrity.description: Verify the agent modified the actual local_rules.xml file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Use of a text editor or standard file redirection commands writing to the correct path (/var/ossec/etc/rules/local_rules.xml), not just a mock file or echoing text without saving.

integrity.description: Verify the agent wrote a functional regular expression rather than just hardcoding exact string matches.
integrity.visual_evidence: The rule definition uses regex syntax (e.g., `|`, `.*`, `(?i)`) rather than multiple literal `<match>` tags for the exact sample strings provided in the prompt.

integrity.description: Verify the agent genuinely restarted or checked the Wazuh manager service.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal output showing the actual system response from wazuh-control or systemctl, not just typed commands without execution.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target file is /var/ossec/etc/rules/local_rules.xml. The rule must have exactly id="100300" and level="10". The regex must be case-insensitive and successfully match strings like 'powershell.exe -enc JAB', 'powershell -EncodedCommand JAB', 'powershell.exe -e JAB', and 'POWERSHELL.EXE -ENCODEDCOMMAND JAB'. It should logically NOT match benign commands like 'powershell.exe -ExecutionPolicy Bypass' or 'powershell.exe -File script.ps1'.
